mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending corporate revenue contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a rolling three-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized contraction in household spending, which compresses forward earnings multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails if joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or if the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: A peak and subsequent decline in industrial production reflects weakening aggregate supply utilization and fading capacity expansion, which reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO breaks below its three-month moving average for more than 70% of the sample period, or if the rolling Spearman correlation exceeds 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the denominator for corporate earnings per share, directly supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth accelerates, or if the predictive R-squared drops below 0.01 over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding a 3.0 percent annualized threshold erodes real purchasing power and forces central bank tightening, which increases equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL annualized growth exceeds 3.0 percent, or if the forward return distribution shows no significant skew.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent movements between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate reveal underlying labor market friction; when payroll growth accelerates while unemployment remains sticky, equity markets price in structural inefficiencies that depress forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE levels shows a positive coefficient in a rolling regression, or if SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below the 1.2 million unit annualized rate signals a collapse in residential investment and wealth effects, which transmits to broader consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where HOUST falls below 1200 thousand units, or if the signal exhibits no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
